input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The local turn-based grid engine under `/app` produces resume audits where opposing sides step through blocked tiles or act from positions the human cannot currently see, but uninterrupted runs stay clean. Running `bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh` writes `/app/output/resume-audit.json`. Bundled layout maps under `/app/data`, action scripts under `/app/data/scripts`, and tables under `/app/config` define the inputs each case name uses; a correct audit reconciles both execution paths against those inputs.

The audit JSON has a top-level cases array for rook, bishop, knight, lancer, sentinel, and warden. Each case uses name, ok, fresh, resume, moves, visibility, effects, records, and log. The fresh and resume summaries describe the same scenario through the two execution paths and carry illegal, steps, and sig. The sig value is the engine signature over the resolved move list for that path, not a new per-run identifier. Each move entry carries turn, side id, from coordinates, to coordinates, kind, and legal.

Records points to a JSONL file whose nonblank rows parse to the same list in the same order. Log points to whitespace-delimited rows in the same order. The audit is clean when every case has ok true, both summaries report illegal 0, signatures match when both paths ran opposing turns, and the JSON, records file, and log file all describe the same resolved rows.

The matrix run also leaves `/app/bin/rogctl` executable. Running `bash /app/scripts/one-case.sh <case>` writes `/app/output/single-<case>.json` with the same top-level cases wrapper and the one requested row, matching that row from the matrix audit.

/app/config/profiles.toml
/app/config/sight.tsv
/app/config/weights.tsv
/app/data/layouts/bishop.map
/app/data/layouts/knight.map
/app/data/layouts/lancer.map
/app/data/layouts/rook.map
/app/data/layouts/sentinel.map
/app/data/layouts/warden.map
/app/data/scenarios.tsv
/app/data/scripts/bishop.tsv
/app/data/scripts/knight.tsv
/app/data/scripts/lancer.tsv
/app/data/scripts/rook.tsv
/app/data/scripts/sentinel.tsv
/app/data/scripts/warden.tsv
/app/docs/architecture.md
/app/docs/operations.md
/app/go.mod
/app/internal/a0/pack.go
/app/internal/b1/cache.go
/app/internal/c2/effect.go
/app/internal/d3/plan.go
/app/internal/e4/native.go
/app/internal/e4/sight.go
/app/internal/f5/run.go
/app/internal/f5/types.go
/app/internal/g6/render.go
/app/internal/h7/stats.go
/app/internal/sim/layout.go
/app/internal/sim/profile.go
/app/internal/sim/types.go
/app/internal/sim/world.go
/app/probe/legality.c
/app/probe/legality.h
/app/probe/list.c

/app/scripts/one-case.sh
/app/scripts/run-matrix.sh
root@f30385e12b3c:/app#

New Terminal Output:

root@f30385e12b3c:/app# cat /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
out="/app/output/resume-audit.json"
work=""
one_case=""
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --out) out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --work) work="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --case) one_case="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    *) shift ;;
  esac
done
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$out")"
make -s -C /app build
args=("--out" "$out")
if [ -n "$work" ]; then
  args+=("--root" "$work")
fi
if [ -n "$one_case" ]; then
  args+=("--case" "$one_case")
fi
/app/bin/rogctl "${args[@]}"
root@f30385e12b3c:/app# cat /app/scripts/one-case.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
if [ "$#" -lt 1 ]; then
  echo "usage: one-case.sh <name>" >&2
  exit 2
fi
name="$1"
out="/app/output/single-${name}.json"
bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh --out "$out" --case "$name"
root@f30385e12b3c:/app#

New Terminal Output:

root@f30385e12b3c:/app# cat /app/Makefile
.PHONY: build clean

build:
        mkdir -p /app/bin
        go build -o /app/bin/rogctl ./cmd/rogctl

clean:
        rm -f /app/bin/rogctl
root@f30385e12b3c:/app# cat /app/README.md
# Grid turn engine

Local workspace for scripted grid scenarios. Build with `make build` and drive cases through `scripts/run-matrix.sh`.
root@f30385e12b3c:/app#
